course_description:
This course develops synthesis skills in relation to regulatory compliance, structural, environmental and construction strategies, and design and construction work processes. Students design integrated structural, environmental and construction solutions for a studio project through activities and assignments that also develop skills in code compliance, teamwork, design detailing and documentation coordination for medium to large-scale buildings.

BLDG3220 Building Systems: Integration and Detailing is one of five technology courses in the Bachelor of Architectural Design. The technology courses develop the structural, constructional and environmental knowledge and skills required of an architect. Theoretical understanding is augmented by learning activities focused on developing the capacity to integrate technical knowledge and skills with design thinking.

BLDG3220 develops students’ knowledge of design development and documentation methods, and skills in the integration of building codes and technology into the design, detailing and documentation process for medium to large-scale (4+ storey) buildings.ﾠStudents also develop familiarity with project planning and team management in preparation for professional experience on completion of the Bachelor of Architectural Design.ﾠBLDG3220 delivers theoretical knowledge and develops practical skills through lectures and workshops supported by a self-directed online construction learning environment that students can access via the Blackboard eLearning website.

BLDG3220 Building Systems: Integration and Detailing addresses the following learning actions associated with the design process:

course_aims: This course aims to familiarise students withﾠdesign development and construction documentationﾠfor medium to large-scale architectural projects.

learning_outcomes.description: identify, analyse and integrate relevant industry legislation, codes and standards,
identify, analyse and integrate appropriate structural, construction and environmental strategies,
collaboratively produce a schematic design proposal,
individually produce a design development report, and
collaboratively produce a set of construction drawings.

assessment_details.title: Assignment 1 - Schematic Design Development at 1:100
assessment_details.due_date: Submission via Blackboard 20/08/2025 4:00 pm Presentation & Discussion in class 20/08/2025
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Assessment Rationale: Students work in a small group to resolve and develop a provided schematic design proposal, including the selection of structural materials and set outs, service strategies and envelope systems and materials based on environmental performance requirement and the requirements of the National Construction Code. Groups must also prepare and submit weekly meeting minutes to track project progress and guide engagement with tutors. The collected meeting minutes are to be submitted with Assignment 1. Group peer assessment tools (BuddyCheck) will provide formative feedback on group performance in Assignment 1 that modifies individual student marks. Group work note: Group work plays a significant part of BLDG3220, and has been incorporated into the subject to help prepare you for working in architectural practice should you decide to take a year out between the Bachelor and Masters courses. Exercises in week 1 of semester will help to form groups and to make clear agreements or contracts between group members about how to communicate, how to share resources, how to give feedback and advice to other group members and the consequences for any group member for not following the group agreement. Group work can be difficult, particularly as the stresses of semester mount and assessment items for other subjects are due. If you find that your group is not functioning effectively please contact the course co-ordinator for support. Assessment task to be completed without use of AI and/or MT: his assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged.
Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission.
To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
If a student wishes to apply for an extension, they must apply online on or before the assignment due date. When possible, it is suggested that requests are submitted 2 business days prior to the submission due date for the assignment to allow processing time. Students with outstanding applications for extensions are advised to submit their assessment by the original due date, irrespective of whether the work is complete, so that what has been done can be graded.
Extensions for group submissions must include the completed group member acknowledgement form with at least 50% of the group agreeing to the extension. This form can be found here: https://my.uq.edu.au/files/35533/extension-to-group-assessment.pdf
Late submission
Standard 10% (or 1 grade) per day for 7 days
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: Standard 10% (or 1 grade) per day for 7 days

assessment_details.title: Assignment 2 - Design Development at 1:20, 1:10 and 1:5
assessment_details.task_description: Assessment Rationale: In Assignment 2 students continue to work with their group from Assignment 1 to further resolve the schematic design proposal, this time at a finer level of detail. Assignment 2 is graded individually, but students continue to work in alignment with the rest of their group , to resolve construction details at 1:20, 1:10 or 1:5 in a way that supports and reinforces the architectural ideas that were presented in Assignment 1. A Detail Development template will be provided as a guide. Formative feedback will be provided in tutorials on work-in-progress and an assignment Q+A session in Week 8. Groups must also prepare and submit weekly meeting minutes to track project progress and guide engagement with tutors. The collected meeting minutes are to be submitted with Assignment 2. Group peer assessment tools (BuddyCheck) will provide formative feedback on group performance in Assignment 2 that does not modify individual student marks. Group work note: Group work plays a significant part of BLDG3220, and has been incorporated into the subject to help prepare you for working in architectural practice should you decide to take a year out between the Bachelor and Masters courses. Exercises in week 1 of semester will help to form groups and to make clear agreements or contracts between group members about how to communicate, how to share resources, how to give feedback and advice to other group members and the consequences for any group member for not following the group agreement. Group work can be difficult, particularly as the stresses of semester mount and assessment items for other subjects are due. Even though this assignment is individual work, the dynamics of your group may impact on your productivity. If you find that your group is not functioning effectively please contact the course co-ordinator for support. Assessment task to be completed without use of AI and/or MT: This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. assessment_details.due_date: 12/11/2025 4:00 pm Submission: via Blackboard A3 Hardcopy Submission: to be delivered to course co-ordinator's office.

assessment_details.task_description: Assessment Rationale: In Assignment 3 students return to working collaboratively with their group from assignments 1 and 2 to produce a set of coordinated and coherent construction drawings for the design they have developed and refined in Assignments 1 and 2. learning_activities.topic: Workshop Week 1 - Course Introduction, team formation and schematic design selection Week 2 - Structural, servicing and environmental strategies 1 Week 3 - Structural, servicing and environmental strategies 2 Week 4 - Assignment 1 submission and review Week 5 - Construction processes and staging, practice detail selection Week 6 - Practice detail development Week 7 - Detail development 1 Week 8 - Detail development 2 Week 9 - Assignment 2 submission and briefing for Assignment 3 Week 10 - Construction Documentation 1 Week 11 - Construction Documentation 2 Week 12 - Construction Documentation 3 Week 13 - Design Week - no class this week Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Field trips Weekly in-class workshops will be supported by site visits to view and examine buildings under construction and completed buildings. Currently three site visits are being planned: the Advanced Engineering Building (concrete framed, window wall building), the Andrew N Liveris Building (concrete framed curtain wall building), 25 King Street, Fortitude Valley (mass timber framed curtain wall building) Dates for site visits will be confirmed as soon as possible. Site visits will be planned for Thursday afternoons. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
